events.title: US Forces Strike Iranian Tankers
events.desc: US forces fired on and disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers attempting to violate an American blockade of Iran's ports. This action, carried out by a US Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet using precision munitions, aimed to prevent the vessels from reaching Iran and follows a report of a third Iranian-flagged vessel being disabled earlier in the week.

events.title: UAE Engages Missile and Drone Threats from Iran
events.desc: The United Arab Emirates reported its air defenses were actively engaging missile and drone threats originating from Iran. This incident represents a further test of the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, occurring shortly after the US military struck two Iranian tankers.

events.title: Israel Strikes Hezbollah Target in Beirut
events.desc: Israel conducted a strike in Beirut, targeting a senior Hezbollah figure. This marks the first such strike in the Lebanese capital since a ceasefire was established in mid-April, potentially reigniting tensions.

events.title: Iran Accuses US of 'Reckless Military Adventure'
events.desc: Iran's representative, Abbas Araghchi, accused the United States of engaging in 'reckless military adventures.' He stated that US attacks occur precisely when diplomatic solutions are being pursued, highlighting a perceived pattern of escalation.

events.title: Iranian Police Infrastructure Targeted
events.desc: An investigation by Bellingcat identified at least 80 police stations or infrastructure related to law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group that have been damaged or destroyed in the initial weeks of the conflict involving the US, Israel, and Iran. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's 'repressive capacity'.

events.title: Two Waves of Bombing on Iran School Compound Verified
events.desc: Bellingcat verified two new videos depicting strikes on an Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) compound and an adjacent school in Minab. The videos, released by Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, show multiple missiles hitting the complex, providing further details on the February incident.

events.title: US Warplane Strikes Iranian Tankers to Enforce Blockade
events.desc: A US warplane disabled two Iranian tankers attempting to violate a port blockade, according to US Central Command. The precision strike aimed to prevent the vessels from reaching Iran, amidst reports of sporadic clashes in the Strait of Hormuz and ongoing diplomatic talks.

events.title: South Korea Investigates Ship Fire in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: South Korean investigators have begun examining the cause of an explosion and fire aboard a Korean-operated vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. The investigation is ongoing amid uncertainty about whether the incident was an attack.

events.title: US Jets Fire on Iranian-Flagged Tankers
events.desc: US jets fired on and disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers that attempted to violate the American blockade of Iran's ports. US Central Command stated that precision munitions were used to prevent the ships from entering Iran.

events.title: US Military Strikes Two Empty Iranian Oil Tankers
events.desc: The U.S. military struck two empty Iranian-flagged oil tankers that were reportedly attempting to violate the U.S. blockade. U.S. Central Command confirmed the action and stated that all three recently targeted vessels are no longer transiting to Iran.

events.title: Pope Asks Leaders to Calm Tensions Amid Iran War
events.desc: Pope Leo, following a meeting with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, prayed for world leaders to calm global tensions and reduce hatred. The Pope, who has previously criticized the Iran war, urged governments to turn away from violence.

events.title: Palestinian Developer Seeks Dismissal of Hamas Support Lawsuit
events.desc: A prominent Palestinian-American developer, Bashar Masri, is asking a U.S. judge to dismiss a lawsuit accusing him of supporting Hamas. Masri argues that the lawsuit equates participation in Gaza's economy with terrorism.
